question: A parallel multi-instance activity has 10 instances and the completion condition `= numberOfCompletedInstances / numberOfInstances >= 0.5`. The 5th instance finishes successfully. What happens to the remaining 5 active instances?

options.text: They are terminated and the multi-instance body completes.
They continue running, and the body waits for all 10 instances to complete before propagating `outputCollection`.
They keep running but their results are dropped from `outputCollection` because the condition was already met.
The completion condition is ignored because `numberOfCompletedInstances` is not exposed as a process variable.

a.text: The `completionCondition` is evaluated each time an instance completes. When it evaluates to `true`, any instances that are still active are terminated and the multi-instance body completes.

b.text: A `completionCondition` exists precisely to short-circuit the wait-for-all behaviour. Once it evaluates to `true`, the body completes without waiting for the remaining instances.

c.text: The remaining instances are terminated, not allowed to keep running with discarded output. They simply stop.

d.text: `numberOfCompletedInstances` and the other BPMN multi-instance properties are available specifically for use in the `completionCondition` expression and take precedence over process variables with the same name.

explanation: Camunda 8.8 re-evaluates the `completionCondition` whenever an instance of the multi-instance body completes. When the boolean expression becomes `true`, every still-active instance is terminated and the body itself completes. The properties `numberOfInstances`, `numberOfActiveInstances`, `numberOfCompletedInstances`, and `numberOfTerminatedInstances` are exposed for use in this expression and shadow process variables of the same name.
